name: Thanksgiving Turkey Marinade - Dkp
recipeInstructions:
1. Thaw the turkey (if frozen) on Monday and Tuesday in the refrigerator.
2. On Wednesday, place 3 onions (cut up), 4 garlic cloves (sliced), and 1 large bunch of parsley in the cavity of the bird.
3. Pour Vernors (ginger ale) and 8 oz orange juice concentrate into the cavity.
4. Cover and rub the bird with Catalina dressing. Sprinkle with seasoned salt, pepper, and paprika.
5. Place the whole mixture in a garbage bag and marinate overnight.
6. Before baking, discard all marinade.
Note: When I finally bought my house, I wanted to have Thanksgiving here instead of at my mother's (which was traditional). I had NEVER done a turkey before. I bought this HUGE creature and started to do the marinade thing (my Mom's recipe). Well, I don't have much counter space and this bird was a slipping and sliding all over the place and I was laughing hysterically. My Mom called to see how everything was going and all she could hear was me cackling. She jumped in her car and came over and here I was, on the floor with this damn bird in my lap and marinade all over me. It was one of those moments...great marinade tho!
